explanations.3: ARG creates a build-time variable that can be overridden when building the image; this one sets a default user ID of 1000.
explanations.13: These ARG lines repeat the same variable names from earlier—they're needed again here so they're available in later build stages after the FROM statement.
explanations.18: The ${GROUP_ID} syntax substitutes the variable value into the command; the -g flag tells groupadd to use that specific numeric group ID.
explanations.19: This useradd command adds a user and assigns them to two groups at once (the one matching their username, plus the 'node' group) so they have permissions for both.
explanations.27: The --chown flag during COPY ensures the copied files are owned by your custom user instead of root, so they can actually edit those files later.
explanations.40: CMD sets the default command to run when the container starts; unlike RUN, this happens every time someone runs the container, not just during the build.
